page: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6a.12xlarge demonstrates strong performance in both single-core and multi-core CPU workloads, as evidenced by stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. Memory performance is robust, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching the top tier, though large-block uncached read and write speeds align with average mid-range performance. Ray tracing and image processing tasks also show strong execution speeds, while software compilation and database operations perform at average levels.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. It offers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6a.12xlarge provides a balanced resource density of compute and memory, making it a viable option for workloads that do not require specialized GPU acceleration or high local storage IOPS. It is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and moderate-scale data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU acceleration but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong performance in multi-core CPU execution, cryptography, and cached memory bandwidth, alongside average performance in software compilation and database operations. The instance offers a balanced resource profile, making it a cost-efficient choice for general-purpose enterprise workloads, web serving, and application servers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor, delivering balanced compute and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-capacity DDR4 memory for enterprise applications.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 system memory
Delivers 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Strong benchmark performance in multi-core processing and cryptography
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for general-purpose enterprise applications and web serving
